title: S&P 500 Breaks 7,000 Barrier for First Time; Nasdaq Longest Win Streak Since 2009
detail: The S&P 500 closed at a record 7,022.95 on April 15 — the first-ever close above 7,000 — posting a 3.3% weekly gain. The Nasdaq recorded its first close above 24,000 (24,016.02) and entered a 12-session winning streak, its longest since 2009. The Dow gained approximately 1% for the week. Japan's Nikkei 225 also hit a record high, rising 2.43% in sympathy with Wall Street gains. The rally defied the grim April 14 IMF WEO (global growth cut to 3.1%), as markets priced in the Iran ceasefire holding and the Strait of Hormuz Green Channel remaining operational. The divergence between record equity highs and the IMF's downgrade reflects markets weighting geopolitical de-escalation over fundamental macro weakness.

media.caption: S&P 500 breaks above 7,000 for the first time amid Iran ceasefire optimism

title: Penn Wharton: China Effective US Tariff Rate at 31.6%; USTR Comment Period Closes
detail: Penn Wharton Budget Model released its April 15, 2026 update of effective US tariff rates, providing the key benchmark ahead of the FOMC April 29-30 stagflation decision. China faces an effective US tariff rate of 31.6% (as of February 2026) following the November 2025 agreement that reduced bilateral tariffs from 104%/84% peaks — that agreement remains in force through November 10, 2026. The April 15 date was also the USTR comment deadline for Section 301 investigations into Chinese excess manufacturing capacity in steel, aluminum, electric vehicles, and semiconductors; hearings are scheduled for April 28, 2026. The tariff data represents critical input for the Fed's inflation projections and for businesses navigating supply chain decisions.

media.caption: Penn Wharton Budget Model releases April 2026 effective tariff rate analysis
